title: heatwave
description: An extreme heatwave creates dangerous working conditions and threatens critical IT infrastructure.

timeline.title: Extreme Heat Advisory
timeline.description: National weather service issues extreme heat advisory. Temperatures forecast to reach 42°C for 5 days.

timeline.title: Cooling System Strain
timeline.description: HVAC systems running at maximum capacity. Datacenter inlet temperatures rising above thresholds.

timeline.title: Staff Welfare Concerns
timeline.description: Non-air-conditioned office areas become uncomfortable. Staff reporting heat exhaustion symptoms.

timeline.title: HVAC Overload
timeline.description: One HVAC unit trips due to overload. Datacenter temperatures rising rapidly.

timeline.title: Grid Demand Warning
timeline.description: Utility company requests voluntary power reduction due to grid strain from peak air conditioning demand.

timeline.title: Heatwave Subsides
timeline.description: Temperatures drop. Systems restored to normal. Review of cooling capacity undertaken.

node-1.title: Extreme Heat Advisory
node-1.situation: The national weather service has issued an extreme heat advisory. Temperatures are forecast to reach 42°C (108°F) for 5 consecutive days. Your facility's HVAC system is rated for conditions up to 38°C ambient. The datacenter is already running at the upper edge of its thermal envelope.
node-1.question: What is your immediate planning action?

options.label: A
options.text: Convene an emergency facilities and IT meeting: assess HVAC capacity limits, identify non-essential IT workloads that can be suspended to reduce heat, plan staff welfare measures, and check generator fuel for potential HVAC failures.

options.label: B
options.text: Continue normal operations — the HVAC system should handle it.

options.label: C
options.text: Shut down all non-essential systems immediately without assessing which systems are truly non-essential.

node-2.title: Staff Welfare
node-2.situation: Temperatures outside have reached 40°C. Several open-plan office areas without air conditioning are reaching 35°C. Two staff members are showing symptoms of heat exhaustion — confusion and excessive sweating. HVAC units are running at full capacity.
node-2.question: How do you address staff welfare?

options.label: A
options.text: Move affected staff to cooled areas immediately, administer first aid and summon medical assistance if needed, allow remote work for staff in uncooled areas, and set up a designated cool rest area with water.

options.label: B
options.text: Open windows to create airflow in uncooled offices and allow staff to use personal fans.

node-3.title: HVAC Unit Trip
node-3.situation: One of three HVAC units has tripped due to thermal overload. Datacenter inlet temperatures are rising at 1.5°C per minute and have reached 32°C. The remaining two HVAC units are at maximum load. A portable cooler is available in the equipment store.
node-3.question: How do you respond to the HVAC failure?

options.label: A
options.text: Deploy the portable cooler to the datacenter hot aisle, begin gracefully suspending non-critical servers to reduce heat generation, reset the tripped HVAC unit if it is safe to do so, and monitor temperatures continuously.

options.label: B
options.text: Keep all servers running to maintain availability and wait for the HVAC technician to arrive.

node-4.title: Grid Demand Reduction Request
node-4.situation: The utility company has issued a voluntary demand reduction request due to grid strain from regional peak air conditioning use. Complying means reducing non-essential electrical loads. Your generator is fuelled and ready but activating it during grid strain events risks overloading the local distribution network.
node-4.question: How do you respond to the grid demand request?

options.label: A
options.text: Reduce non-essential electrical loads (lighting, non-critical equipment) to comply with the voluntary request, while maintaining HVAC and critical IT systems. Do not activate the generator during the grid strain event.

options.label: B
options.text: Activate the generator to take the facility off-grid and eliminate the need to reduce loads.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The heatwave has subsided. Staff welfare measures prevented any serious heat-related illness. The tripped HVAC unit has been serviced and the datacenter returned to normal thermal operating conditions. A review of cooling capacity and redundancy is now being commissioned.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
